label: Pitch deck
body:
## Active skill: pitch-deck

Emit a PitchDeckSpec. Aim for 10 slides in this canonical order — adapt
content to what the snapshot supports, but keep this narrative shape:

  1. Title       — layout: 'title'    — company name + one-line value prop
  2. Problem     — layout: 'content'  — bullets from the Problem stage; lead with the most concrete pain
  3. Solution    — layout: 'content'  — what we do, in 2-3 bullets; one explicit before/after
  4. Why now     — layout: 'content'  — market/regulatory/tech shift that makes this unblocked TODAY
  5. Market      — layout: 'stats'    — TAM/SAM/SOM as stat blocks; cite the 'market' stage hashes
  6. Competition — layout: 'content'  — how we win the lane (positioning, not feature comparison)
  7. Business model — layout: 'content' — pricing, unit economics, motion (PLG / SLG / hybrid)
  8. Traction    — layout: 'stats' or 'content' — pilots, MRR, retention, signed LOIs; specifics over adjectives
  9. Team        — layout: 'content'  — why these founders for this problem; relevant prior wins only
  10. Ask        — layout: 'closing'  — round size, runway it buys, milestones at the end of that runway

Slide density rules:
- Title and closing slides: 1 line of body (or none — let the title carry it).
- Content slides: ≤ 4 bullets, each ≤ 14 words. Long prose loses an investor in 2 seconds.
- Stat slides: one headline number per stat block; supporting context goes in 'sub'.
- Speaker notes are mandatory for every non-title slide. They're the founder's
  prompt during the live read; include the source stage tag (e.g. 'PROBLEM-001 v3')
  so a re-validate pass can trace the claim back.

verifyClaims:
- One entry per concrete claim the deck makes that an investor could (and
  should) check independently: market sizes, traction numbers, retention,
  pricing benchmarks. Not every bullet — only the trust-load-bearing ones.
- 'method' must be specific. 'check market data' is useless; 'IBISWorld
  SMB accounting category 2026 + Pitchbook deal data 2024-2026' is useful.

Voice:
- Confident, not boastful. Past tense for traction; present for vision.
- No buzzwords ('disruptive', 'revolutionary', 'AI-powered'). The artefact
  has to survive a skim by a partner who's seen 200 decks this quarter.
